title: Marsdenia purpusiana
taxonomy: Marsdenia purpusiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae and the genus Marsdenia. This species is native to Mexico and Central America. Marsdenia purpusiana is a climbing plant that grows in tropical forests and woodlands. It is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which includes other plants like Periwinkle and Oleander.
uses: Marsdenia purpusiana is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Research suggests that Marsdenia purpusiana may have therapeutic applications in treating various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential benefits and uses of Marsdenia purpusiana in modern medicine.

side_effects: Marsdenia purpusiana, also known as the Mexican climbing milkweed, contains toxic compounds that can be hazardous to humans and animals if ingested. These toxic compounds, similar to those found in other milkweed species, can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain if ingested in large amounts. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to cardiac problems, including arrhythmias and decreased cardiac output. While the plant has potential medicinal uses, its toxicity requires careful handling and caution. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Marsdenia purpusiana.
